user.title: Connect to ML2MQTT
user.description: Enter the reachable ML2MQTT app URL. You only need to configure this once, then add or manage models from the integration options.

data_description.app_url: Use `http://workspace:5000` in the devcontainer setup so Home Assistant can reach the app from inside Docker.

mode.title: Initial model setup
mode.description: Choose whether to bind one existing model or create the first model under this ML2MQTT integration.

bind_existing.title: Bind existing model
bind_existing.description: Choose an existing ML2MQTT model and the Home Assistant entities that should feed it.

data_description.model_id: Pick an ML2MQTT model that already exists in the app.
data_description.source_entities: Select the Home Assistant entities whose states should be sent to the model when they change.

create_new.title: Create and bind model
create_new.description: Create a new model from Home Assistant entities. ML2MQTT will auto-generate the MQTT topic and helper entities for you.

data_description.model_name: Used for the ML2MQTT model name, helper entities, and the default MQTT topic.
data_description.labels: Enter one label per line or separate them with commas. These become the choices in the Current Label select.
data_description.source_entities: Select the Home Assistant entities whose states should be used as the model inputs.

advanced.name: Advanced options
advanced.description: Optional overrides for the generated MQTT topic and the numeric replacement used when a source entity has no state.

data_description.mqtt_topic: Leave empty to let ML2MQTT generate `ml2mqtt/<model-name>` automatically.
data_description.default_value: Used only when a selected entity reports `unknown`, `unavailable`, or no value.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the ML2MQTT app API.
error.invalid_labels: Add at least two possible predictions.
error.model_not_found: The selected model is no longer available.

abort.already_configured_service: This ML2MQTT app URL is already configured. Use Configure on the existing integration to add more models.
abort.no_available_models: No available ML2MQTT models were found for this action.

init.title: Manage ML2MQTT models
init.description: Add more models under this ML2MQTT integration, update the shared app URL, or remove models you no longer want in Home Assistant.

update_url.title: Update ML2MQTT app URL
update_url.description: Change the shared ML2MQTT app URL used by all models under this integration.

data_description.app_url: Use `http://workspace:5000` in the devcontainer setup so Home Assistant can reach the app from inside Docker.

add_existing.title: Add existing model
add_existing.description: Add another existing ML2MQTT model under this integration and bind Home Assistant source entities to it.

data_description.model_id: Pick an ML2MQTT model that is not already managed by this integration.
data_description.source_entities: Select the Home Assistant entities whose states should be sent to the model when they change.

create_new.title: Create and add model
create_new.description: Create a new ML2MQTT model under this integration.

data_description.model_name: Used for the ML2MQTT model name, helper entities, and the default MQTT topic.
data_description.labels: Enter one label per line or separate them with commas. These become the choices in the Current Label select.
data_description.source_entities: Select the Home Assistant entities whose states should be used as the model inputs.

advanced.name: Advanced options
advanced.description: Optional overrides for the generated MQTT topic and the numeric replacement used when a source entity has no state.

data_description.mqtt_topic: Leave empty to let ML2MQTT generate `ml2mqtt/<model-name>` automatically.
data_description.default_value: Used only when a selected entity reports `unknown`, `unavailable`, or no value.

rebind_model.title: Update model inputs
rebind_model.description: Change which Home Assistant entities are sent into one of the models managed by this integration.

data_description.model_id: Choose the managed ML2MQTT model you want to update.
data_description.source_entities: Select the Home Assistant entities whose current states should be sent to the model.

remove_model.title: Remove managed model
remove_model.description: Remove one model from this Home Assistant integration and clear its ML2MQTT binding.

data_description.model_id: Choose the model to remove from this integration.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the ML2MQTT app API.
error.invalid_labels: Add at least two possible predictions.
error.model_not_found: The selected model is no longer available.

abort.no_available_models: No available ML2MQTT models were found for this action.
abort.no_configured_models: This integration is not currently managing any ML2MQTT models.
